title: Türkiye Mobilizes Over 300 Experts to Launch National Quantum Technology Strategy and Superconducting Processor Programs - Quantum Computing Report
gist: Turkey's Secretariat of Defence Industries (SSB) launched a sovereign national quantum strategy (OTAG process, 305 experts, 123 institutions, 85 priority tech areas) with ASELSAN as prime contractor for a domestic superconducting QPU fab, plus the Kerteriz quantum-magnetometer navigation project for GPS-denied environments and a national platform to counter 'harvest now, decrypt later' cryptographic threats.

themes.theme: Sovereign quantum strategy as defense doctrine

insights.claim: Turkey's Defence Industries Secretary Haluk Görgün framed dependency on foreign quantum technology stacks as equivalent to a silent transfer of national sovereignty, justifying a state-directed quantum industrial policy.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Frames quantum tech as a national-security asset class in Turkish policy, implying sustained state funding regardless of near-term commercial ROI — relevant for assessing durability of the program.
insights.quote: which Defence Industries Secretary Haluk Görgün designated as a critical vulnerability equivalent to a silent transfer of national sovereignty.
insights.locator: The implementation blueprint targets 85 priority technology areas

insights.claim: The SSB Quantum Program synthesized input from 305 technical experts across 123 defense, academic, and military organizations under the OTAĞ (Quantum Focus Technology Network) process, targeting 85 priority technology areas split into 34 short-term and 51 long-term benchmarks.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Scale of coordination (305 experts/123 institutions) signals a serious, well-resourced state-led program rather than a symbolic announcement — a template comparable to other national quantum strategies (US, China, EU) worth benchmarking against.
insights.quote: the strategic infrastructure plan synthesizes contributions from 305 technical experts across 123 defense, academic, and military organizations.

insights.claim: Turkey aims to position itself within the NATO Transatlantic Quantum Community while simultaneously pursuing sovereign, domestically manufactured quantum hardware, indicating a dual-track strategy of alliance integration and independence.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: A NATO member building sovereign quantum capability rather than relying solely on allied technology transfer is a geopolitical signal about intra-alliance tech competition and self-reliance trends.
insights.quote: positioning the nation to protect its data sovereign boundaries inside the NATO Transatlantic Quantum Community.
insights.locator: This workforce initiative leverages the exceptionally young engineering base

insights.claim: ASELSAN was awarded the prime contract for Turkey's Superconducting Quantum Processor Unit (QPU) Development Project, building on R&D initiated in 2021 and the 5-qubit QuanT prototype deployed at TOBB ETÜ, and will build the country's first dedicated superconducting production facility.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: ASELSAN becomes the investable/trackable proxy for Turkey's quantum hardware ambitions; a 5-qubit prototype-to-fab transition timeline gives a concrete benchmark to judge program progress against peers.
insights.quote: Building upon foundational R&D tracks initiated in 2021 and the deployment of the 5-qubit QuanT prototype system at TOBB ETÜ, the project transitions the domestic quantum ecosystem from laboratory design into industrial manufacturing.
insights.locator: Domestic Manufacturing and Superconducting Processor Development Projects

insights.claim: The domestic superconducting supply chain links ASELSAN's manufacturing with Sabancı University and Koç University for materials/physics modeling and the TÜBİTAK National Metrology Institute (UME) for calibration and interoperability standards, forming a full-stack co-design network.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: A mapped institutional network (industry-academia-metrology) shows how Turkey is structuring vertical integration, a pattern comparable to NIST/SRI's QMEC model in the US — useful for cross-country benchmarking of quantum industrial policy design.
insights.locator: To sustain a localized, full-stack hardware supply chain

themes.theme: Quantum sensing for GPS-denied military operations

insights.claim: The Kerteriz Project is developing quantum magnetometers for precision submarine detection and signal-independent navigation, exploiting phase sensitivity to micro-Tesla magnetic distortions to enable geographic tracking in GPS-denied or electronic-warfare-congested zones.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Quantum sensing for GPS-denial resilience is a near-term (pre-fault-tolerant) military application with direct battlefield relevance — a segment where commercial deployment could arrive well before general-purpose quantum computing.
insights.quote: By exploiting the localized phase sensitivities of quantum states to measure micro-Tesla ambient magnetic distortions, the system enables naval and aerospace platforms to maintain precise geographic tracking in GPS-denied or highly electronic-warfare-congested operating zones.
insights.locator: Quantum Sensing and Inertial Navigation in Denied Environments

insights.claim: Turkey's quantum program explicitly targets 'harvest now, decrypt later' attacks against legacy RSA/ECC public-key encryption, running an SSB Quantum Algorithm Competition and a Strategic Competence Development Cooperation Protocol to build post-quantum cryptography workforce capacity.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Confirms post-quantum cryptography migration urgency is being treated as an active national-security priority by a NATO member state, reinforcing the broader global timeline pressure on RSA/ECC-reliant systems.
insights.quote: To counter long-range cryptographic threats—such as the “harvest now, decrypt later” vector targeting legacy public-key encryption (RSA and ECC)
insights.locator: The operational synchronization of these diverse platforms

insights.claim: The Turkish defense sector's engineering workforce averages just 34 years of age, a demographic asset the SSB explicitly plans to leverage to build a specialized quantum engineering talent pipeline.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: A young defense-engineering workforce is a structural advantage for rapid talent scaling in an emerging field — a factor differentiating Turkey's program pace from countries facing aging technical workforces.
insights.quote: This workforce initiative leverages the exceptionally young engineering base of the Turkish defense sector—averaging 34 years of age
insights.locator: This workforce initiative leverages the exceptionally young engineering base

insights.claim: The newly deployed Türkiye Quantum Platform portal serves as the central hub managing shared access to state-funded nanofabrication lines, cryogenic testing systems, and sensor packaging labs across the national program.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Centralized shared-infrastructure model (rather than fragmented lab-by-lab investment) suggests capital efficiency in Turkey's approach — a governance pattern to compare against other national programs' infrastructure-sharing models.
insights.quote: an inter-institutional hub designed to manage the shared utilization of state-funded nanofabrication lines, cryogenic testing systems, and sensor packaging laboratories.
insights.locator: The operational synchronization of these diverse platforms
